other_findings.finding: VE3 forms approximately 2% of endothelial cells in healthy adult dermis
VE3 expands significantly in AD and psoriasis lesional skin
Fetal skin VE cells align transcriptionally with adult VE3
SNCG+PECAM1+ VE3 cells form distended vascular structures in superficial dermis by whole-mount immunostaining

evidence_quotes.quote: Three distinct states of PECAM1(CD31)-expressing vascular endothelial cells (VE1, VE2, VE3) are present in adult dermis. VE3, which forms ~2% of endothelial cells, is characterized by gamma synuclein (SNCG) and high expression of the venular capillary marker ACKR1
VE3 co-express inflammatory cytokines, chemokines and leukocyte adhesion molecules including IL6, IL33, SELE and ICAM1
we observed significant expansion of VE3 in AD and psoriasis lesional skin
